nodes.title: US Popular Music Since 1989
nodes.description: An exploration of the sounds and social politics of US popular music over the last three decades. Focuses on the transformations wrought by digital technologies to the production, consumption, and politics of popular music. Through examination of songs, music videos, and writings, students refine critical listening and writing skills.

nodes.title: Hist Western Classical Music
nodes.description: Study of Western classical music from 1300 to the present, exploring music from various stylistic periods from a historicist perspective and examining how music history aligns with broader concepts, theories, and beliefs circulating at a particular moment in time. Prerequisites: MU 1310, MU 2320, or MU 2310.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Post-19c Theory Lab
nodes.description: Intensive study of solfege, extended tonality and atonality at the keyboard, dictation, and score reading. Prerequisite: MU 2314 or Instructor permission. Co-requisite: MU 3313.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Theory/Practice Jazz Improv I
nodes.description: Basic repertory, idiomatic usage, aural skills, theoretical constructs, and strategies for the jazz improvisor. Prerequisites: MU 2320 or MU 2310 or Instructor permission; intermediate instrumental skill.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; intermediate instrumental skill. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Blues & Related Traditions
nodes.description: Traces the development of blues from African origins to modern blues, its rural and urban social contexts, and relation to African-American history and culture. Cross-listed with: CRES 1813.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Intro to Music Technology
nodes.description: Introductory overview of music technology. Study of acoustic physics, history of music technology, basic hardware set up, computerized music notation, Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) electronic music production, and music video creation. Prerequisite: MU 1310 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements:
Instructor permission required
PACE students by permission and override
All students must fill out this form to be considered for instructor permission. No one can register without competing it:https://forms.gle/G4Ai6PeWsfrwqQXo6

nodes.title: Studio Production I
nodes.description: Explores the fundamentals of music studio recording production. Topics include recording hardware, ProTools software, microphone technique, signal processing, and post production engineering. Prerequisites: MU 1770 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Post-19c Theory and Practice
nodes.description: Study of extended tonality, atonality, and 12-tone techniques. Examples drawn from 20th and 21st century literature. Music majors take MU 3314 concurrently. Prerequisite: MU 2313 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Music majors take MU 3314 concurrently.

nodes.title: Music Business and Copyright
nodes.description: Survey of basic concepts and practices in music business including copyright, licensing, publishing, contracts, marketing, agencies, unions and guilds, and career development. Prerequisite: A Catamount Core WIL1 course.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum sophomore standing; Music majors with concentration in Music Technology and Music Technology and Business minors only during week of registration; Open to PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: History of Rock and Roll
nodes.description: Examines rock music as a succession of related musical styles and as a social movement reflecting and influencing the changing American political and social landscape.

nodes.title: Senior Recital
nodes.description: The solo recital is the capstone performance experience for music majors. Repertoire for the recital will be chosen in consultation with the private lesson teacher and, where appropriate, the area head. Students should take MUL 4500 concurrently. Prerequisites: Music or Music Education majors only; Students must have performed on at least four Student Performance Recitals before they are eligible for this course.
nodes.other_requirements: Music or Music Education majors only; Students must have performed on at least four Student Performance Recitals before they are eligible for this course. MUS, MUSE majors only; Must pass Level IV Exam; Lab fee $260 per credit

nodes.title: Percussion Techniques
nodes.description: Class instruction of various orchestral pitched and unpitched percussive instruments including materials and procedures for teaching these instruments in the elementary and secondary schools. Pre/Co-requisite: MU 1550.

nodes.title: Teaching Internship Seminar
nodes.description: Companion course to supervised field work, giving students experience in specialized areas for their professional development. It is designed to provide context to the field work, resources for effective planning and teaching, and assist in developing the Vermont Licensure Portfolio and achieving InTASC standards. Prerequisite: Senior standing. Co-requisite: MU 4552.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing.

nodes.title: Studio Production III
nodes.description: Explores professional techniques of music studio production. Topics include recording hardware, signal processing, Digital Audio Workstations, and post production engineering (mixing and mastering). Prerequisite: MU 2772.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission

nodes.title: Arranging for Jazz Orchestra
nodes.description: Composing and arranging for big band. Practice in techniques of jazz arranging and study of historic works. Final project is jazz standard arranged for big band, read by the UVM Jazz Ensemble. Prerequisite: MU 2320 or MU 2313 or instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission

nodes.title: Harmony and Form II
nodes.description: Study of chromatic harmony (applied chords, modulation) and small forms (binary, ternary, variation). Music majors take MU 2314 concurrently. Prerequisite: MU 2320 or MU 2310 or Instructor permission. Co-requisite: Music majors taking MU 2313 to fulfill a theory requirement also take MU 2314 Harmony and Form Lab II concurrently.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Arts Management
nodes.description: Focuses on the business of presenting the performing arts. Topics include: planning, marketing, logistics and operations of non-profit arts organizations. Prerequisite: A Catamount Core WIL1 course.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Brass Techniques
nodes.description: Class instruction on trumpet, trombone, and horn including materials and procedures for teaching these instruments in elementary and secondary schools. Pre/Co-requisite: MU 1550.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Vocal Techniques
nodes.description: Foundation course in applied singing, and in teaching singing. Intended for students in music education, and students intending to teach private singing lessons or lead choirs. Pre/Co-requisite: MU 1550.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Intro to Teaching Music
nodes.description: Introduction to the foundational principles, philosophies, opportunities, issues, and roles inherent to teaching music in various contexts and settings including school, community, and private settings. Open to all music majors and non-majors who are interested in teaching people through their love of music.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Creating Music for Video
nodes.description: Students will score short films using digital audio software. Emphasis is on 4-5 scoring projects, with additional background reading and written critiques. Prerequisites: MU 1770.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Jazz Harmony Lab
nodes.description: Musical skills will be sharpened through singing prepared and unprepared material, through practice of rhythmic exercises, and through melodic, harmonic, and rhythmic dictation. Practice in the use of solfege syllables from the moveable do system to aid successful sight singing. Co-requisite: MU 2320.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Live Sound Reinforcement
nodes.description: Overview of techniques and tools used in amplification of live sound performance in music, theater, and dance. Study of physical properties of sound, fundamentals of acoustics, and current technology and equipment.
nodes.other_requirements: Music majors with a concentration in Music Technology and Business and Music Technology and Business minors only during the week of registration; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission Required

nodes.title: Internship: Student Teaching
nodes.description: Teaching Interns will work under the guidance of their Licensed Music Mentor and University Supervisor to become committed reflective practitioners, instructional leaders and change agents, collaborating with other professionals to make a positive difference in schools and in the lives of all learners. Prerequisites: MUL 4550; Music Education majors only; Senior Standing; admission to student teaching; minimum GPA both overall and in professional courses 3. Co-requisite: MU 4551.
nodes.other_requirements: Music Education majors only; Senior Standing; admission to student teaching; minimum GPA both overall and in professional courses 3.

nodes.title: Music Theory Fundamentals
nodes.description: Fundamentals of music notation, rhythm, melody, scales, and harmony. A course for non-majors or for students preparing to enter MU 2320 or MU 2310.
nodes.other_requirements: Music and Music Ed majors and Music, Musical Theatre and Music Technology & Business minors only

nodes.title: Senior Project: Comp/Theory
nodes.description: Research paper or composition/analysis; topic chosen under direction of a faculty member. Prerequisite: Composition/Theory concentration; Senior standing; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Composition/Theory concentration; Senior standing; Instructor permission. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Harmony and Form Lab II
nodes.description: Intensive study of solfege, intermediate keyboard harmony, and dictation. Students should also register for MU 2313. Prerequisites: MU 2311 or MU 2321. Co-requisite: MU 2313.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Theory/Practice Jazz Improv II
nodes.description: Chord substitution, re-harmonization, scale alteration, free" improvisation, and other techniques in written assignments and classroom performance of modern jazz repertory. Prerequisites: MU 2327, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. MU3327 is a capstone course offered every other year for experienced jazz players. The class involves intensive coaching in real-time improvisation and interaction with a rhythm section (bass, drums, piano, guitar). We spend a good portion of the class working on applied theory exercises (on our instruments or voice) and then we try to apply these concepts with each student taking a turn playing solos with the rhythm section. Chord substitution, re-harmonization, scale alteration, "free" improvisation, and other techniques are explored in written assignments and classroom performance of modern jazz repertory. Regular attendance and practice playing or singing class materials. Any student with at least an intermediate experience performing jazz and basic knowledge of music theory or jazz harmony is welcome to join class irrespective of prerequisites. Please contact instructor if you are unsure wether you are qualified.

nodes.title: Studio Production II
nodes.description: Explores advanced techniques of music studio production. Topics include recording hardware, signal processing, Digital Audio Workstations, and post production engineering (mixing and mastering). Prerequisite: MU 2771.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Harmony and Form Lab I
nodes.description: Intensive study of solfege, elementary keyboard harmony, and dictation. Students should also register for MU 2310. Prerequisite: Ability to read music and to sing or play a musical instrument at elementary level. Co-requisite: MU 2310.
nodes.other_requirements: Ability to read music and to sing or play a musical instrument at elementary level. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Intro to Western Music
nodes.description: A survey of musical styles from antiquity to the present drawing from the Western concert and other traditions.

nodes.title: Jazz History Styles & Analysis
nodes.description: An in-depth survey of jazz from early New Orleans to contemporary styles. Work includes close listening, study of transcriptions, and stylistic analysis. Final projects. Prerequisites: MU 2320 or MU 2310.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: General Music Methods
nodes.description: Methodologies, lesson planning, assessment, and standards-based curriculum development for general music at the elementary and secondary school levels. Pre/co-requisites: MU 1550; acceptance into licensure program in Music Education.
nodes.other_requirements: acceptance into licensure program in Music Education

nodes.title: Jazz Harmony
nodes.description: Study of jazz harmony, including essential harmonic progressions, turnarounds, chord substitutions, and melody harmonization. Music majors with concentration in Jazz Studies or Music Technology and Business take MU 2321 Jazz Harmony Lab concurrently. Prerequisite: MU 1310 or equivalent music theory fundamentals proficiency. Co-requisite: MU 2321 is required for Music majors with a concentration in Jazz Studies or Music Technology and Business and is encouraged for all others enrolled in MU 2320.
nodes.other_requirements: Music majors with concentration in Jazz Studies or Music Technology and Business take MU 2321 Jazz Harmony Lab concurrently. Placement test. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Senior Project: MTB
nodes.description: Project utilizes current music technology. Topic chosen under direction of faculty member. Prerequisites: MU 3771; Music Technology and Business concentration; Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Music Technology and Business concentration; Senior standing. Contact instructor for meeting times and location; Senior standing in Music Technology Concentration; Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Harmony and Form I
nodes.description: Study of diatonic melody and harmony, phrase structure, and elaborative techniques. Music majors are required to take MU 2311 concurrently. Prerequisite: MU 1310 or equivalent music theory fundamentals proficiency, determined by placement test. Co-requisites: MU 2311 is required for Music majors and encouraged for all enrolled in MU 2310.
nodes.other_requirements: Music majors are required to take MU 2311 concurrently. PACE students by permission and override. Students should begin with basic knowledge of notation and elements of music theory as determined by the department placement exam. Anyone needing more support may be referred to MU 1310 (Music Theory Fundamentals). Concurrent enrollment in Class Piano or private piano lessons is not required, but strongly recommended. Concurrent enrollment in Harmony and Form Lab I is required for all music majors, except for MTB majors who began prior to Fall 2021. Non-majors and upper-class MTB majors are also encouraged to enroll.

nodes.title: Choral Music Methods
nodes.description: Standards-based curriculum development, lesson planning, repertoire selection, rehearsal techniques, and assessment strategies for teaching choral music at the elementary and secondary school levels. Pre/Co-requisite: MU 1550; acceptance into licensure program in Music Education; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: acceptance into licensure program in Music Education; or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Practicum in Teaching Music
nodes.description: Supervised field experience in music education. Prerequisites: MU 1550. Pre/Co-requisites: MU 3550, MU 3552, MU 3554.
nodes.other_requirements: Prerequisite enforced by the system:MU 1550 See link to syllabus for details

nodes.title: Music&Cult:New Orleans
nodes.description: Examines the interrelationships between styles of music in New Orleans and the cultures that support them.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students
